title: Hacking AI Eyes: Vision Model Attacks

modules.title: How Vision Models Process and Trust Multimodal Inputs
modules.sub: Understanding how AI systems interpret and implicitly trust visual data
modules.description: Learners explore the internal mechanics of vision-language models, examining how they parse images, screenshots, and documents as trusted input sources. They map the trust assumptions baked into multimodal pipelines and identify where those assumptions create exploitable gaps for attackers.

modules.title: Anatomy of a Multimodal Prompt Injection: From Image to Command
modules.sub: Tracing how hidden image instructions hijack AI model behavior
modules.description: Learners dissect the structure of a multimodal prompt injection attack step by step, from embedding a malicious instruction inside an image to watching the model execute unintended commands. They analyze real injection samples to understand what makes a payload effective and what signals betray its presence.

modules.title: Real-World Attack Chains: OCR Exploits, QR Payloads, and Adversarial Overlays
modules.sub: Examining proven attack vectors that weaponize visual input channels
modules.description: Learners investigate documented attack chains that exploit OCR pipelines, encode commands inside QR codes, and use adversarial overlays to manipulate model perception. They evaluate each technique's threat severity and assess which production environments are most exposed to each vector.

modules.title: Detection Strategies: Input Validation and Output Monitoring for Vision Pipelines
modules.sub: Building controls that catch injections before and after model inference
modules.description: Learners design input validation routines that screen visual content for embedded instructions before it reaches a vision model. They also configure output monitoring strategies to flag anomalous or policy-violating model responses that may indicate a successful injection attempt.

modules.title: Securing Multimodal Deployments: Architecture Hardening and Trust Boundaries
modules.sub: Designing system architectures that isolate and constrain vision model authority
modules.description: Learners apply defense-in-depth principles to multimodal AI architectures, drawing explicit trust boundaries that limit what a vision model can perceive, invoke, or influence downstream. They produce hardened deployment diagrams that reflect least-privilege principles for AI perception pipelines in production.

modules.title: Red Team Lab: Crafting and Catching Multimodal Injection Payloads
modules.sub: Hands-on offensive and defensive exercises against live vision pipelines
modules.description: Learners take turns as attacker and defender, crafting multimodal injection payloads targeting a sandboxed vision-enabled system and then switching roles to detect and block those same attacks. They document findings in a structured red team report that captures exploited weaknesses and recommended remediations.

modules.title: Policy and Vendor Controls for Production Vision-Enabled Systems
modules.sub: Translating technical risk into governance requirements and vendor accountability
modules.description: Learners draft security policy requirements for organizations deploying vision-enabled AI, covering data handling, acceptable use, and incident response obligations. They also evaluate vendor documentation and API configurations to determine whether third-party vision services meet the trust and control standards required for production deployment.

synopsis: When AI systems can see, attackers learn to hide malicious instructions inside images, screenshots, and documents — turning your AI's perception into an attack surface. This course dissects multimodal prompt injection techniques so security professionals can identify, test for, and mitigate these threats before they reach production.
rationale: Enterprise adoption of vision-capable AI agents is accelerating while defensive tooling and awareness lag significantly behind the emerging multimodal attack surface.
learning_outcome: Learners can design input validation controls and threat models for vision-enabled AI pipelines and run targeted red team exercises to expose injection vulnerabilities.
source_signals: Multi-modal prompt injection image attacks against GPT-4V
Hacking AI is TOO EASY (this should be illegal)
